name: Crispy Roasted Potatoes
description: Extra-crispy oven-roasted potatoes started on a screaming-hot preheated sheet pan.
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds baby or Yukon Gold potatoes, cut into 1 1/2- to 2-inch chunks
1 sprig fresh rosemary (or 1 teaspoon dried), leaves chopped
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions: Place a heavy rimmed baking sheet on a middle rack and preheat the oven to 425°F with the pan inside for at least 20 to 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, toss the potato chunks with the olive oil, chopped rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Carefully pour the potatoes onto the hot pan and spread into a single layer. Roast, shaking the pan or stirring once, until well browned and fork-tender, 25 to 30 minutes.
author: The Kitchn

ai.cliffNotes: Preheating the sheet pan means the potatoes sear on contact, so they crisp instead of steam.
keyTips: Get the pan ripping hot before the potatoes touch it — that first sizzle is what makes them crispy.
Cut the potatoes evenly and spread them in a single layer with space between, so they brown rather than steam.
